name: Lee Collins
abstract: Leeds "Lee" Collins (October 17, 1901 – July 3, 1960) was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Bill Bailey, Won't You Please Come Home?
I'm Busy and You Can't Come In

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: And I played with Lee Collins and them. That’s where I graduate to big time. I joined Lee Collins and David Jones’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Lee, yes. I played with Lee, with the, uh, Earl Humphrey's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Emmanuel Sayles
about_quotes.evidence: And he had hired a band, they was already out there, that was Lee Collins. ... It was a Lee Collins group, and he had, uh, Lee Collins on trumpet... I took John Morell's play. ... Lee Collins on trumpet. ... Binge got the idea of taking the show and, and building this band with, uh, with Lee Collins

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Lee Collins, of course, was another trumpet in the band. So Bunk sat right, of course, beside Lee, and they used the same horn. They passed the horn back and forth.

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Humphrey
about_quotes.evidence: me and Lee Collins did? We went over there to Mandeville. We played over there at the hotel. Me and Lee had the very name. We played for the hotel over there.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: we sent him and got Lee when Red Allen came in the band... We were on two trumpets... He stayed with us about three or four, about a month.
